description: KCP Observability Expansion - Add recursive workspace metrics, API binding topology, lifecycle monitoring, controller/server dashboards, alerting rules, audit logging via OTel, and OTLP push support

userStories.title: Create KCP Controller Health Grafana dashboard
userStories.description: As an operator, I want a Grafana dashboard showing KCP controller workqueue health so that I can detect controllers falling behind on reconciliation.
acceptanceCriteria: Create observability/grafana/dashboards/kcp-controller-health.json with Grafana dashboard JSON model
Dashboard uid is 'kcp-controller-health', title is 'KCP Controller Health', tags are ['kcp', 'controllers']

Panel 1: Queue Depth by Controller - stacked area, PromQL: workqueue_depth{job=~".*kcp.*", name=~"apibinding|apiexport|workspace|logicalcluster|replication|apiexportendpointslice|workspacetype"}, thresholds >50 warning, >100 critical
Panel 2: Processing Rate - lines, PromQL: sum(rate(workqueue_adds_total{job=~".*kcp.*", name=~"apibinding|apiexport|workspace|logicalcluster|replication"}[5m])) by (name), unit ops/s
Panel 3: Retry Rate - lines, PromQL: sum(rate(workqueue_retries_total{job=~".*kcp.*"}[5m])) by (name), thresholds >5 warning, >10 critical
Panel 4: Queue Wait Latency p99 - lines, PromQL: histogram_quantile(0.99, sum(rate(workqueue_queue_duration_seconds_bucket{job=~".*kcp.*"}[5m])) by (le, name))
Panel 5: Work Duration p99 - lines, PromQL: histogram_quantile(0.99, sum(rate(workqueue_work_duration_seconds_bucket{job=~".*kcp.*"}[5m])) by (le, name)), thresholds >10s warning, >30s critical
Panel 6: Unfinished Work Duration - lines, PromQL: workqueue_unfinished_work_seconds{job=~".*kcp.*"}, thresholds >120s warning, >300s critical
Panel 7: Longest Running Processor - lines, PromQL: workqueue_longest_running_processor_seconds{job=~".*kcp.*"}
All panels use datasource {"type": "prometheus", "uid": "prometheus"} and legend {{name}}
Panels organized in 4 rows: Queue Health Overview, Error Indicators, Detailed Timing, Longest Running
Add a ConfigMap in setup.sh named grafana-dashboard-kcp-controller-health in namespace observability with label grafana_dashboard: '1', containing the dashboard JSON
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Dashboard-only task, no Go code changes. Uses existing workqueue_* metrics already scraped from KCP pods. See todos/01-controller-workqueue-dashboard.md for full panel specs.

userStories.title: Add KCP API Group breakdown panels to Server Health dashboard
userStories.description: As an operator, I want API server metrics broken down by KCP API groups so that I can identify which API group is generating the most traffic or errors.
acceptanceCriteria: Modify observability/grafana/dashboards/kcp-server-health.json to add 2 new rows after the existing panels
All 7 existing panels remain unchanged with their original panel IDs
New panel IDs start from 100 to avoid conflicts

New Row 'KCP API Groups' with 4 panels (each width 12): Request Rate by Group (stacked area), Request Rate by Resource Top 10 (lines), Watch Event Rate (lines), Error Rate by Group (lines with thresholds >1 warning, >10 critical)

New Row 'KCP Operation Latency' with 4 panels (each width 12): Workspace CRUD Latency p50/p95/p99 by verb, APIBinding Creation Latency p50/p95/p99, LIST Request Latency by Resource p99, WATCH Connection Count by Resource
All new panels filter on KCP API groups: group=~"tenancy.kcp.io|apis.kcp.io|core.kcp.io|topology.kcp.io|cache.kcp.io"
All new panels use datasource {"type": "prometheus", "uid": "prometheus"}
Each new row is a type 'row' panel with collapsed: false
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Dashboard edit only. Adds 8 new panels to existing dashboard. See todos/03-kcp-api-group-breakdown.md for exact PromQL queries per panel.

userStories.title: Add shard metrics collection to Go exporter
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to monitor KCP shard health so that I can detect when a shard goes not-Ready and an entire segment of workspaces becomes unreachable.
acceptanceCriteria: Add shardItem struct to kcp-exporter/main.go parsing metadata.name, spec.baseURL, and status.conditions
Add condition struct with Type, Status, LastTransitionTime, Reason, Message fields (reused by later stories)
Register kcp_shards_total gauge, kcp_shard_info gauge vec (labels: name, url, ready), kcp_shard_condition gauge vec (labels: name, type, status)
Implement collectShards() function querying /clusters/root/apis/core.kcp.io/v1alpha1/shards
collectShards resets shard_info and shard_condition gauges before setting new values to handle shard removal
For each shard, set shard_info with ready='true' if Ready condition is True, otherwise ready='false'
For each shard condition, set shard_condition with value 1
Call collectShards() from the existing collectMetrics() function
go build ./... passes in kcp-exporter directory
go vet ./... passes in kcp-exporter directory
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follows the existing doRequest() + JSON parse + gauge set pattern. See todos/05-additional-kcp-resources.md and todos/08-shard-health-monitoring.md for full type definitions.

userStories.title: Add workspace type metrics collection to Go exporter
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to see what workspace types are defined in KCP so that I can detect unexpected changes to the workspace taxonomy.
acceptanceCriteria: Add workspaceTypeItem struct parsing metadata.name, spec.defaultChildWorkspaceType, spec.initializer (bool), spec.terminator (bool)
Register kcp_workspace_types_total gauge and kcp_workspace_type_info gauge vec (labels: name, has_initializer, has_terminator)
Implement collectWorkspaceTypes() querying /clusters/root/apis/tenancy.kcp.io/v1alpha1/workspacetypes
Reset workspaceTypeInfo gauge before setting new values
Format initializer/terminator booleans as 'true'/'false' strings for label values
Call collectWorkspaceTypes() from collectMetrics()
go build ./... passes in kcp-exporter directory
go vet ./... passes in kcp-exporter directory
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Same pattern as collectShards. See todos/05-additional-kcp-resources.md for type definitions and expected metric values.

userStories.title: Add APIExportEndpointSlice metrics collection to Go exporter
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to monitor APIExport endpoint slices so that I can detect when a virtual workspace serving an APIExport is down (zero endpoints).
acceptanceCriteria: Add endpointSliceItem struct parsing metadata.name, spec.export.name, spec.export.path, status.endpoints[].url
Register kcp_apiexport_endpoint_slices_total gauge and kcp_apiexport_endpoints_total gauge vec (labels: export, workspace)
Implement collectEndpointSlices() querying /clusters/root/apis/apis.kcp.io/v1alpha1/apiexportendpointslices
Reset apiExportEndpointsTotal gauge before setting new values
Set kcp_apiexport_endpoints_total to len(status.endpoints) for each slice - value 0 means virtual workspace is down
Call collectEndpointSlices() from collectMetrics()
go build ./... passes in kcp-exporter directory
go vet ./... passes in kcp-exporter directory
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Same pattern as collectShards and collectWorkspaceTypes. See todos/05-additional-kcp-resources.md for full spec.

userStories.title: Add workspace lifecycle duration and stuck detection metrics
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to know how long workspaces have been in their current phase so that I can detect workspaces stuck in Scheduling, Initializing, or Terminating.
acceptanceCriteria: Add workspaceLifecycleItem struct with metadata.name, metadata.creationTimestamp, status.phase, status.conditions[] (reuse condition type from US-003)
Register kcp_workspace_phase_duration_seconds gauge vec (labels: path, phase) and kcp_workspace_stuck_count gauge vec (labels: phase)
Implement calculatePhaseDuration() that returns seconds: for Ready phase use lastTransitionTime of Ready condition, for non-Ready use creationTimestamp as fallback
Define stuckThresholds map: Scheduling=5min, Initializing=10min, Terminating=15min
Reset both gauges before each collection cycle to clear stale data from deleted workspaces
Accumulate stuck counts per phase based on threshold comparison and set stuck count gauges
Integrate into existing workspace collection in collectMetrics() - reuses the existing workspace list API response, no additional HTTP request needed
Clamp negative durations to 0 to handle clock skew
go build ./... passes in kcp-exporter directory
go vet ./... passes in kcp-exporter directory
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Reuses existing workspace API call. Parse creationTimestamp and condition lastTransitionTime as time.RFC3339. See todos/07-workspace-lifecycle-metrics.md.

userStories.title: Add recursive workspace discovery types and function
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the workspace tree discovery mechanism so that per-workspace metrics can be collected for all child workspaces, not just root.
acceptanceCriteria: Add WorkspaceNode struct with Name, Path, Type, Phase string fields and Children []*WorkspaceNode
Add workspaceFullItem struct parsing metadata.name, spec.type.name, status.phase
Implement discoverWorkspaces(ctx, client, cfg, parentPath) returning ([]*WorkspaceNode, error)
discoverWorkspaces queries /clusters/{parentPath}/apis/tenancy.kcp.io/v1alpha1/workspaces
Build child path as parentPath + ':' + ws.Metadata.Name
Only recurse into workspaces with status.phase == 'Ready'
If recursion into a child fails, log warning and skip that subtree - do not fail the whole tree
go build ./... passes in kcp-exporter directory
go vet ./... passes in kcp-exporter directory
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is the foundation for US-008 and US-009. See todos/04-recursive-workspace-metrics.md for complete function signatures and recursive pattern.

userStories.title: Add per-workspace collection with concurrency and main integration
userStories.description: As an operator, I want per-workspace resource counts collected for all workspaces in the tree so that org-alpha, org-beta, org-gamma resources are visible in metrics.
acceptanceCriteria: Register kcp_workspace_info gauge vec (labels: path, name, type, phase), kcp_workspace_apiexports_total (labels: workspace_path), kcp_workspace_apibindings_total (labels: workspace_path, phase), kcp_workspace_apiresourceschemas_total (labels: workspace_path), kcp_workspace_tree_depth gauge, kcp_workspace_children_total gauge vec (labels: path)
Implement collectWorkspaceMetrics(ctx, client, cfg, tree, sem) that walks the tree and queries each Ready workspace for apiexports, apibindings, apiresourceschemas
Use semaphore channel with capacity 10 (make(chan struct{}, 10)) to limit concurrent API queries
Use sync.WaitGroup for goroutine coordination
Add discovery goroutine to main() with 5-minute ticker, protected by sync.RWMutex on workspaceTree variable
Run discovery once immediately on startup before first ticker
Add collection ticker at 60-second interval that reads workspace tree under RLock
Reset all per-workspace GaugeVecs before each collection cycle to clear stale entries from deleted workspaces
go build ./... passes in kcp-exporter directory
go vet ./... passes in kcp-exporter directory
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-007 for discoverWorkspaces() function and WorkspaceNode type. See todos/04-recursive-workspace-metrics.md for goroutine integration pattern and collectForWorkspace details.

userStories.title: Add APIBinding topology metrics and collection
userStories.description: As an operator, I want to see provider-consumer relationships from APIBindings so that I can understand which workspaces consume APIs from which providers.
acceptanceCriteria: Add apiBindingFullItem struct parsing metadata.name, spec.reference.export.path, spec.reference.export.name, status.phase, status.boundResources, status.conditions
Add apiExportFullItem struct parsing metadata.name, spec.latestResourceSchemas, status.identityHash, status.conditions
Add exportKey struct with Workspace and Name string fields
Register kcp_apibinding_info gauge vec (labels: consumer_workspace, provider_workspace, export_name, phase)
Register kcp_apiexport_consumers_total gauge vec (labels: provider_workspace, export_name)
Register kcp_apiexport_info gauge vec (labels: workspace, name, identity_hash, schema_count)
Implement collectTopology(ctx, client, cfg, tree) that walks the workspace tree collecting bindings and exports
Implement collectBindingsForWorkspace() querying /clusters/{path}/apis/apis.kcp.io/v1alpha1/apibindings, accumulating consumer counts per exportKey
Implement collectExportsForWorkspace() querying /clusters/{path}/apis/apis.kcp.io/v1alpha1/apiexports
Call collectTopology() from the 60-second collection loop after collectWorkspaceMetrics()
Reset all topology metrics before each collection cycle
Use 'unknown' as default identity_hash when empty
go build ./... passes in kcp-exporter directory
go vet ./... passes in kcp-exporter directory
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-008 for workspace tree. See todos/06-apibinding-topology.md for full type definitions, collection logic, and demo scenario.

userStories.title: Add topology, lifecycle, and shard panels to KCP Resources dashboard
userStories.description: As an operator, I want Grafana dashboard panels showing API sharing topology, workspace lifecycle durations, and shard health alongside existing resource count panels.
acceptanceCriteria: Modify observability/grafana/dashboards/kcp-resources.json to add 3 new rows

Add 'API Sharing Topology' row with: table panel showing kcp_apibinding_info (columns: Provider, Export, Consumer, Phase via labelsToFields transform), stat panel for count(kcp_apibinding_info), stat panel for bound ratio percentage, stat panel for unique exports consumed

Add 'Workspace Lifecycle' row with: gauge panel showing max by (phase) (kcp_workspace_phase_duration_seconds{phase!='Ready'}) with green/yellow/red thresholds, stat panel for kcp_workspace_stuck_count > 0 with red highlight
Add 'Shard Health' row with: stat panel for kcp_shard_condition{type='Ready', status='True'} mapped to Healthy/Unhealthy, table panel for kcp_shard_info, stat panel for kcp_shards_total
All existing panels remain unchanged
New panels use datasource {"type": "prometheus", "uid": "prometheus"}
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-003 (shard metrics), US-006 (lifecycle metrics), US-009 (topology metrics) existing in the exporter. See todos/06-apibinding-topology.md, todos/07-workspace-lifecycle-metrics.md, todos/08-shard-health-monitoring.md for panel specs.

userStories.title: Enable AlertManager and create KCP PrometheusRule
userStories.description: As an operator, I want automated alerts for KCP failure domains so that I am notified when workspaces get stuck, API error rates spike, controllers back up, etcd approaches limits, or the exporter fails.
acceptanceCriteria: Modify observability/prometheus/values.yaml to enable alertmanager with resources (50m CPU, 64Mi-128Mi memory), 24h retention, null receiver, group_by alertname+severity
Create observability/prometheus/kcp-alerts.yaml as a PrometheusRule with label release: kube-prometheus-stack
Group kcp-workspace-lifecycle: 3 rules - KCPWorkspaceSchedulingStuck (>0 for 5m), KCPWorkspaceInitializingStuck (>0 for 10m), KCPWorkspaceTerminatingStuck (>0 for 15m)
Group kcp-api-health: 2 rules - KCPAPIServerErrorRate (5xx rate >1% for 5m, critical), KCPAPIBindingsUnbound (phase!=Bound >0 for 10m)
Group kcp-controller-health: 3 rules - KCPControllerQueueBacklog (depth >100 for 10m), KCPControllerHighRetryRate (retries >10/s for 5m), KCPControllerStuck (unfinished_work >300s for 5m, critical)

Group kcp-etcd-health: 4 rules - KCPEtcdDatabaseSizeLarge (>6GB, critical), KCPEtcdLeaderChangesFrequent (rate >0.5 over 15m), KCPEtcdHighFsyncLatency (p99 >500ms), KCPEtcdHighBackendCommitLatency (p99 >1s)
Group kcp-exporter-health: 2 rules - KCPExporterScrapeErrors (rate >0.1 for 5m), KCPExporterDown (up==0 for 2m, critical)
Every alert has severity label (warning or critical), summary annotation, and description annotation with runbook steps
Add 'kubectl apply -f observability/prometheus/kcp-alerts.yaml' to setup.sh after the Prometheus Helm install step
Total: 14 alert rules across 5 groups
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: YAML config only. The release: kube-prometheus-stack label is critical for Prometheus operator to pick up the rules. See todos/02-kcp-alerting-rules.md for full alert specifications and runbooks.

userStories.title: Create KCP audit policy and configure audit logging
userStories.description: As a platform admin, I want KCP audit events captured in a structured JSON log so that tenant lifecycle, API sharing, and security events have an audit trail.
acceptanceCriteria: Create kcp/audit-policy.yaml with audit.k8s.io/v1 Policy: RequestResponse level for tenancy.kcp.io workspaces create/delete, Metadata level for apis.kcp.io resources, Metadata level for rbac resources, None level for events/endpoints/leases get/list/watch, Metadata default for everything else

Add audit flags to kcp/values.yaml under kcp extraFlags or extraArgs: --audit-policy-file=/etc/kcp/audit/audit-policy.yaml, --audit-log-path=/var/log/kcp/audit.log, --audit-log-format=json, --audit-log-maxsize=100, --audit-log-maxbackup=3
Add volume and volumeMount in kcp/values.yaml for audit-policy ConfigMap (readonly at /etc/kcp/audit) and audit-logs hostPath (at /var/log/kcp)
Add ConfigMap creation to setup.sh: kubectl create configmap kcp-audit-policy --from-file=audit-policy.yaml=kcp/audit-policy.yaml -n kcp --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f -
ConfigMap creation step runs before KCP Helm install so the ConfigMap exists when KCP pods start
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Config/YAML only. The KCP Helm chart v0.14.0 may use kcp.extraArgs or kcp.extraFlags - check chart values. See todos/09-kcp-audit-via-otel.md for audit policy and volume mount details.

userStories.title: Add filelog receiver to OTel Collector for KCP audit events
userStories.description: As a platform admin, I want KCP audit events ingested by the OTel Collector so that they flow through the observability pipeline with structured workspace attributes.
acceptanceCriteria: Add filelog receiver to observability/otel-collector/values.yaml config.receivers section: include /var/log/kcp/audit.log, start_at: end
Add json_parser operator with timestamp parse from requestReceivedTimestamp

Add move operators to extract: annotations.kcp.io/path -> workspace_path, annotations.tenancy.kcp.io/workspace -> logical_cluster, verb -> audit_verb, objectRef.resource -> audit_resource, objectRef.apiGroup -> audit_api_group, user.username -> audit_user, responseStatus.code -> response_code
Add severity_parser mapping response codes: 5xx -> error, 4xx -> warn, 2xx -> info
Update logs pipeline receivers to include filelog alongside otlp: [otlp, filelog]
Add extraVolumes hostPath /var/log/kcp (type DirectoryOrCreate) and extraVolumeMounts at /var/log/kcp (readOnly: true) to collector values
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: OTel Collector config only. Depends on US-012 having enabled KCP audit logging to /var/log/kcp/audit.log. See todos/09-kcp-audit-via-otel.md for operator chain and severity mapping.

userStories.title: Add OTel SDK dependencies and initialize meter provider
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Go exporter to initialize an OTel meter provider so that metrics can be pushed via OTLP to the OTel Collector.
acceptanceCriteria: Add OTel SDK dependencies to kcp-exporter: go.opentelemetry.io/otel, go.opentelemetry.io/otel/sdk, go.opentelemetry.io/otel/sdk/metric, go.opentelemetry.io/otel/exporters/otlp/otlpmetric/otlpmetricgrpc, go.opentelemetry.io/otel/metric, google.golang.org/grpc
Run go mod tidy to update go.mod and go.sum
Implement initOTelMeterProvider(ctx) returning (*sdkmetric.MeterProvider, error)
Read OTLP endpoint from OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT env var, default to opentelemetry-collector.observability.svc.cluster.local:4317
Create OTLP gRPC exporter with insecure connection
Create resource with service.name=kcp-exporter and service.version=0.1.0
Create periodic reader with 30-second interval
In main(), only initialize OTel if OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT is set, log warning and continue with Prometheus-only if init fails
Add graceful shutdown: defer mp.Shutdown() with 5-second timeout context
go build ./... passes in kcp-exporter directory
go vet ./... passes in kcp-exporter directory
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: First part of OTLP refactor. The meter provider is not used by any instruments yet - that comes in US-015. See todos/10-otlp-push-refactor.md for import paths and initialization code.

userStories.title: Create async gauge instruments and dual-write logic
userStories.description: As a developer, I want OTel async gauge instruments that report the same values as the Prometheus gauges so that metrics flow through both paths simultaneously.
acceptanceCriteria: Add exporterState struct with sync.RWMutex protecting int64 fields: workspaceCount, apiExportCount, apiBindingCount, apiResourceSchemaCount, logicalClusterCount
Implement registerAsyncGauges(meter, state) that creates Int64ObservableGauge for each metric with callback reading from state under RLock
OTel metric names use dots: kcp.workspaces.total, kcp.apiexports.total, kcp.apibindings.total, kcp.apiresourceschemas.total, kcp.logical_clusters.total
Add Int64Counter for kcp.exporter.scrape_errors.total and Float64Histogram for kcp.exporter.scrape_duration_seconds
Update collectMetrics() to store current values in exporterState under Lock (dual-write: Prometheus gauges still set as before)
Record scrape duration histogram and increment scrape error counter via OTel instruments when errors occur
Only create OTel instruments if meter provider was successfully initialized (feature-flag via env var)
go build ./... passes in kcp-exporter directory
go vet ./... passes in kcp-exporter directory
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-014 for meter provider. Async gauges match Prometheus gauge semantics exactly - no delta tracking needed. See todos/10-otlp-push-refactor.md for registerAsyncGauges pattern.

userStories.title: Update deployment manifest for OTLP configuration
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the exporter deployment to include OTLP environment variables so that the OTel push path is enabled in-cluster.
acceptanceCriteria: Add OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT env var to kcp-exporter/manifests/deployment.yaml with value opentelemetry-collector.observability.svc.cluster.local:4317
Add OTEL_SERVICE_NAME env var with value kcp-exporter
Add OTEL_RESOURCE_ATTRIBUTES env var with value service.namespace=kcp,deployment.environment=local
Existing Prometheus scrape endpoint (/metrics on port 8080) and ServiceMonitor remain unchanged for backward compatibility
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Small config change. Both Prometheus scrape and OTLP push paths will be active. The prometheusremotewrite exporter in the OTel Collector converts OTel dot-separated names to Prometheus underscore format automatically. See todos/10-otlp-push-refactor.md for deployment YAML.
